_note: Header-driven column map. Columns are located by matching each table's header row against 'headerPatterns' (first match wins), so ONE config handles differing layouts — e.g. the 6-column cm sheet (| Item # | Item Description | Material | Height (cm) | Width (cm) | Weight (kg) |) and the 11-column metres sheet (| # | Item Description | Category | Material | Height (m) | Width (m) | Depth (m) | Unit Weight (kg) | Quantity | Line Volume | Line Weight |). The fixed 'columns' indices are a fallback for required fields when no header matches and for tables with no usable headers. Length unit is auto-detected per table from the dimension headers ('(cm)' / '(m)'); 'inputUnit' is the fallback. 'decimalSeparator' is '.' (English/US: dot decimal) — integers parse identically, so it is safe for the cm sheet too; set to ',' for Italian/European sources. 'code' points at the description so categoryPatterns match item text. Depth/quantity are optional: when the sheet has no such column they are absent (depth derived from mass÷density, quantity defaults to 1). Fragility is handled separately in Stage 2.
